text.en: Reassure the patient; immobilise the bitten limb at heart level; remove rings/bangles.
Apply pressure-immobilisation bandage; do NOT apply a tourniquet, do NOT cut, suck, or wash the wound.
Transport quickly and quietly; minimise muscular movement.
Communicate to PHC/CHC: prepare polyvalent ASV per National Snakebite Protocol.

rules.rationale: ASV must be given as early as possible; pressure-immobilisation slows venom spread, traditional remedies (cutting, sucking, plant pastes) are contraindicated.

text.en: Wash the wound thoroughly with soap and running water for at least 15 minutes.
Apply povidone-iodine or 70 % alcohol after washing.
Refer to ARV centre today; categorise per WHO/MoHFW (I/II/III) for ARV ± RIG.
Update tetanus immunisation if last dose > 5 years.

rules.rationale: Category II/III animal bites require post-exposure prophylaxis with anti-rabies vaccine; rabies is invariably fatal once symptomatic.

text.en: Reassure; immobilise the limb; apply cold compress.
Refer to PHC/CHC for prazosin and observation.

rules.rationale: Indian red scorpion stings can cause severe autonomic storms; oral prazosin under observation reduces mortality.
